issue_title: Stable RNG and Poisson noise
issue_body:
I've found an interesting issue when trying to generate simulated images with the same noise realizations. Due to the way generating Poisson noise works, it uses an indeterminate number of random values per generation, and this number depends on the lambda of the distribution. What this means is, if I take two different noise-free images and add Poisson noise to them with the same seed, the noise realizations will look completely different (even in regions where the initial images are the same).

See the attached files for two images with only slightly different galaxies, and the difference between them:
[pois_noise_diff.zip](https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/files/862244/pois_noise_diff.zip)

I'm using the CCDNoise class here, so read noise is applies entirely after Poisson noise. It should at least be possible to make read noise stable if it's generated first in this class, which is at least a partial solution. As for the Poisson noise, I'm not sure there's a perfect solution, simply due to the nature of a Poisson distribution - if a pixel has an expected extra 0.1 e- hitting it, the generated number can't simply be 0.1 more. The best ideal I can think of would be if pixels with the same value would always get the same Poisson deviate, and if the same pixel is changed between images, the change in the deviate is proportional. The former part is at least theoretically attainable, but I don't know about the latter.

A compromise might be to put in a "stable_rng" option for the CCDNoise class which would result in it using Gaussian noise for each pixel with the correct sigma. It's not quite the right distribution, but it might be good enough for many use cases.

issue_title: Update CSP docs with instructions for those using NPM packages
issue_body:
Hi

On this page there are instructions for how to avoid issues with Font Awesome on a site that specifies a Content Security Policy:
https://fontawesome.com/how-to-use/performance-and-security#content-security-policy

However the current instructions are only aimed at users who download the assets manually, rather than those using the NPM packages with a bundler like webpack.

Would it be possible to document:
1) which package/file is equivalent to the existing file reference (`/static/vendors/fontawesome/css/fa-svg-with-js.css`) - I'm guessing something like `'@fortawesome/fontawesome/styles.css'` from the `@fortawesome/fontawesome` package?
2) how to specify `autoAddCss: false` in JS
3) that users must remember to extract CSS from the bundle
4) that the "Web Fonts with CSS" case does not require these workarounds (it's not explicitly stated currently)

At a guess the example in the docs would look something like:

```js
import '@fortawesome/fontawesome/styles.css';
import fontawesome from '@fortawesome/fontawesome';

// Make sure this is before any other `fontawesome` API calls
fontawesome.config = {
  autoAddCss: false
}
```

My intuition is that the set of people who use CSP is much more likely to overlap with those using a bundler than those still manually managing assets - so making the steps clearer for this group would be really helpful.

Many thanks :-)

issue_title: DOC: tower-cli is API version specific // "Error: The requested object could not be found."
issue_body:
Hi

I hope this helps somebody trying to figure out the next problem.

I'm using ansible tower 3.1.5 (the latest at this time of writing)

I was getting an "Error: The requested object could not be found." error whilst running any command, even

>  tower-cli version

which doesn't suggest what might be the problem...

After digging, it turns out this is because tower-cli uses v2 API although ansible tower only exposes v1 of the API.

It would be great this is checked on startup. Maybe this is documented but I have not found it. 

Digging further seems that I should be using a tower-cli utility pre-v3.2.0. At least the next commit suggests https://github.com/ansible/tower-cli/commit/dba969f93b2f77aa91c09c1eca82a1fb43d6dd86#diff-b8911f2ee40d5bbc1374042a18a21d7f 

I checked the versions pre-v3.2.0 

> pip install ansible-tower-cli==

And installed v3.1.8

> pip install ansible-tower-cli==3.1.8

which fixed the issue

issue_title: parsing f-strings -- opening brace gets duplicated when backslash is followed by an expression
issue_body:
with Python 3.6.0 and typed-ast 1.0.2, I get the following:

```
#!/usr/bin/env python3.6

import typed_ast.ast3 as ast

code1 = '''"\\{x}"'''
code2 = '''f"\\{x}"'''

tree1 = ast.parse(code1, mode='eval')
print(ast.dump(tree1))
tree2 = ast.parse(code2, mode='eval')
print(ast.dump(tree2))
```

output:

```
Expression(body=Str(s='\\{x}'))
Expression(body=JoinedStr(values=[Str(s='\\{'), FormattedValue(value=Name(id='x', ctx=Load()), conversion=-1, format_spec=None)]))
```

Therefore, the normal string is `'\\{x}'`.
But the f-string has two parts: `'\\{'` and an expression `Name(id='x', ctx=Load())`.

Where does the `{` in the string part of f-string come from? This happens also with built-in ast. I can't believe this is the intended behavior... Is it?

issue_title: Schedule load_replication_slots when postgres is restarted during pause
issue_body:
Scenario:

    patronictl pause mycluster
    # stop postgres on master, restore backup, start postgres
    # This removes the replication slot for standbynode
    patronictl reinit mycluster standbynode
    # Master thinks standbynode still has a replication slot, master will not create a new one
    # standbynode cannot replicate

Workaround is to restart postgres via Patroni. It would be nice to schedule reloading of replication slots if we discover that postgres has been restarted.

issue_title: Passing scale to Optimizer changes gradient clipping threshold
issue_body:
Trainers accept an initial learning and a decay parameter. To control the learning rate dynamically without using the built-in decay, one can pass a ```scale``` parameter to the ```trainer.update(scale)``` function. Unfortunately, this directly affects gradient clipping:

From https://github.com/clab/dynet/blob/master/dynet/training.cc#L68:

```
if (scale * gg > clip_threshold) {                              
   ++clips;                                                      
   ++clips_since_status;                                         
   gscale = clip_threshold / (scale * gg);                       
}  
```                                                             

Using ```trainer.update(scale)``` to manage the learning rate, ```clip_threshold``` is effectively divided by ```scale```. Though there might be a reason for this, I was surprised by it. To get the same results as I would with TensorFlow, I have to compensante for this by calling ```trainer.set_clip_threshold(clip_threshold * scale)``` before calling ```update(scale)```.

issue_body:
I've received some feedback asking how to take a schema from the client library and save it to a JSON file. One reason to do this is the [`bq` command-line tool](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/bq-command-line-tool#creatingtablefromfile) accepts a schema file, formatted like

```
[
  {"name": "name", "type": "string", "mode": "required"},
  {"name": "gender", "type": "string", "mode": "nullable"},
  {"name": "count", "type": "integer", "mode": "required"}
]
```

Note: this format is the same as the API representation.

It would be great if our client libraries could read/write in this format.

An example repo where the above commands can be run to verify is available here: https://github.com/gaqzi/pants-require-empty-build-file

This issue is a followup from a conversation with @jsirois on the Slack channel. :)

issue_title: Test caching only works for 1 shard in TravisCI
issue_body: It appears to be the case that the local artifact cache, which is cached by Travis via our `.travis.yml` cache configuration, is thrashed, with only 1 shard "winning". As such we only get the benefit of test caching on 1 shard and Travis throughput is hurt. Investigate a bit more and consider propping up a proper remote artifact cache if there is no solution for fixing the local artifact cache thrash.

issue_title: Port IsolatedProcess implementation to rust, and align with bazel remoting API
issue_body:
Isolated processes currently work for the purposes of testing, but there are lots of abstraction leaks: in particular, a `_Snapshots` snapshots object is exposed which the python code uses to reverse engineer the location of snapshots.

Instead, process execution should be ported to rust (maybe using [duct](https://docs.rs/duct/0.8.1/duct/)?) to remove that leak.

Additionally, the outcome of discussion around https://docs.google.com/document/d/1jGq34ds_fhRLPDnmHNV_FHcJiJnbhmk2XEwOf9-w5To/edit#heading=h.ylneg9wnwxcs was that we should adapt the process execution model (which is implicitly also a "remote process execution" model) more closely with the draft of [bazel's process execution model](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1AaGk7fOPByEvpAbqeXIyE8HX_A3_axxNnvroblTZ_6s/edit#heading=h.ole76l21af90).

issue_title: UnicodeError Gunicorn generate_latest multiprocess
issue_body:
Hi all,

We are currently seeing the following error from our prometheus endpoint:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/cats/server/app_log.py", line 37, in _apply_logging
    result = fn(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/cats/server/prometheus_views.py", line 14, in prometheus_view
    output = generate_latest(registry)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/prometheus_client/exposition.py", line 63, in generate_latest
    for metric in registry.collect():
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/prometheus_client/core.py", line 97, in collect
    for metric in collector.collect():
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/prometheus_client/multiprocess.py", line 24, in collect
    d = core._MmapedDict(f)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/prometheus_client/core.py", line 335, in __init__
    for key, _, pos in self._read_all_values():
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/prometheus_client/core.py", line 365, in _read_all_values
    yield encoded.decode('utf-8'), value, pos
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xa0 in position 102: invalid start byte
```

Weirdly, at first the endpoint responds with our metrics as expected, then after a short period of time as requests come in we start to get the above.  We are finding it impossible to debug what character is causing the issue.  

We only have two metrics:

```
METRICS['application_latency'] = Histogram('application_latency',
                                           "Metrics for application latency",
                                           ['resourceName', 'method', 'worker'])



METRICS['application_responses'] = Counter(
    'application_responses',
    'Metrics for application responses per endpoint, method and http response code',
    ['resourceName', 'method', 'status', 'worker'])
```

And increment them like so:

```
        METRICS['application_responses'].labels(resourceName=resource_name, method=method, status=status_code,
                                                worker=str(os.getpid())).inc()

        METRICS['application_latency'].labels(resourceName=resource_name, method=method,
                                              worker=str(os.getpid())).observe(response_time)
```

All the labels are just strings and the most complex is resourceName which looks like "service.my_method" for example.  Any pointers on how we could possibly diagnose this would be great as currently we are completely stuck.  
We have followed the gunicorn multiprocess example exactly.

Our gunicorn conf is:

```
PROMETHEUS_METRICS = '/prometheus-metrics'

bind = '0.0.0.0:9077'
worker_class = 'gevent'
workers = 8
worker_connections = 8000
worker_tmp_dir = '/worker-tmp'
backlog = 16384
keepalive = 8


def on_starting(_server):
    [os.remove("{}/{}".format(PROMETHEUS_METRICS, f)) for f in os.listdir(PROMETHEUS_METRICS)]

def child_exit(_server, worker):
    from prometheus_client import multiprocess
    multiprocess.mark_process_dead(worker.pid)
```

We also set this environment variable before starting:

```
prometheus_multiproc_dir=/prometheus-metrics
```

Finally, we are running kubernetes hosted on AWS.

Hy, i would like to add a drawing canvas into a table and then a picture into the canvas. Afterwards i planed to manually add other shapes and the canvas helps to keep them aligned. But unfortunately the canvas function is not implemented. 

I found a reference the documentation here: "drawing canvas" and "LockedCanvas"

issue_title: Apple Music using #AlbumView causes crashes
issue_body:
Apple Music has a number of issues with Sonos, and this one seems to also affect the Windows Desktop Controller. 

When you Queue up an entire album, Soco fails on the first track change with an unknown UPNP type of object.container.album.musicAlbum.#AlbumView

Since I convert everything down to regular dicts in my app, it's very frustrating every time a new service is added which does something new.  Incidentally, the use of #AlbumView is even breaking the Windows controller, causing the player to stop and the queue to jump to the first track and loop the first 1-2 seconds over and over.  So this is clearly not just a Soco issue but rather a larger implementation issue with Sonos and Didl.

I'm assuming this has the same attributes as musicAlbum, so I monkeypatched data_structures with this which seems to resolve it for me, but I'm not sure if there are better ways to handle the continously changing Didl datastructures.  

class DidlMusicAlbumView(DidlAlbum):

    """Class that represents a music library album. """

    item_class = 'object.container.album.musicAlbum.#AlbumView'
    # According to the spec, all musicAlbums should be represented in
    # XML by a <container> tag. Sonos sometimes uses <container> and
    # sometimes uses <item>. Set the tag type to '' to indicate that
    # either is allowed.
    tag = ''
    # name: (ns, tag)
    # pylint: disable=protected-access
    _translation = DidlAudioItem._translation.copy()
    _translation.update(
        {
            'artist': ('upnp', 'artist'),
            'genre': ('upnp', 'genre'),
            'producer': ('upnp', 'producer'),
            'toc': ('upnp', 'toc'),
            'album_art_uri': ('upnp', 'albumArtURI'),
        }
    )

issue_title: Thread doesn't exit on vader
issue_body: * ricecooker version: 0.6.10 * Python version: 3.5.3 (/data/sushi-chef-tessa/venv/bin/python) * Operating System: GNU/Linux Ubuntu 14.04.5 LTS ### Description When running a chefs on `vader` it doesn't exit in the end, just hangs around. The issue seems to be related to the use of a separate thread to report progress to sushibar. Here is what I see when the chef is running with remote reporing: If I disable sushibar reporting by specifying a non-functional SUSHIBAR_URL `export SUSHIBAR_URL="http://localhost:8011"` then ricecooker will not try to do the remote reporting and so we see just one thread: and it exists cleanly. ### Where the code? Here is where the websocket reporting thread is started: https://github.com/learningequality/ricecooker/blob/6ade813257760f505e21b2fb212f4b51bb1713d2/ricecooker/sushi_bar_client.py#L164 Here is the place where sushibar client is supposed to `join` the thread before exiting: https://github.com/learningequality/ricecooker/blob/6ade813257760f505e21b2fb212f4b51bb1713d2/ricecooker/sushi_bar_client.py#L99

issue_title: Support for duration in assume_role function
issue_body: assume_role function found in opinel/utils/credentials.py does not support DurationSeconds parameter, unlike the init_sts_session parameter. This creates some issues when using Scout2 to scan large AWS accounts, as the default duration for the credentials using assume_role is only 1 hour, which may not be sufficient for scanning of a large AWS account. Suggest that assume_role should, like init_sts_session, take in a parameter for duration with a large default value.

issue_title: Version 3.0.x is very slow to save image
issue_body:
As mentioned in the comment: https://github.com/docker/docker-py/issues/1352#issuecomment-363907057 the change to use generator (with chunk size set to `1`) in the image save method (applied in https://github.com/docker/docker-py/commit/388f291b13fca76f4974a1ee89225ff7f3afb85b#diff-cc05c8e27ac99930dc2a96358a502480) instead of directly exposing the response object makes it impossible to export bigger images.

Previously we could define the chunk size depending on the use case. We are dealing with big images (GB's of data) and not being able to specify the chunk size makes it impossible to use new version of the client library. I do see it as a regression.

This issue was also mentioned in #1352.

issue_title: ~/.docker/config.json overrides DockerClient.login()
issue_body:
If I use the cli docker tools to log into a custom registry (say AWS ECR) it generated a ~/.docker/config.json entry and caches the creds.

If the creds in the ~/.docker/config,json files are stale, and I use DockerClient.login or DockerClient.images.push with the auth_config parameter, the new credentials are ignored and the python library reads the creds in config.json which are stale.
So the push fails as long as the config.json file exists.

The current workarround is to delete the config.json file before doing a DockerClient.login

issue_title: each graph algorithm should verify correctness before return
issue_body: The DWave_NetworkX graph kernels (MIS, min vertex coloring, ...) do not confirm that the results from the underlying sampler are logically valid in the graph context; i.e., MIS does not confirm that its result is actually an independent set.  Stipulating that the underlying sampler is probabilistic and may return invalid answers, callers of DNX will expect to receive back valid answers in the graph context.

issue_title: Argument Parser in Examples fails to work out of the box
issue_body:
[nidmm_measurement.txt](https://github.com/ni/nimi-python/files/1710665/nidmm_measurement.txt)
Examples such as nidmm_measurement.py make use of the argument parser `argparse`, 

 but for the common user who  just wants to get started with a Python call from the command prompt, you are given an error.
`usage: nidmm_measurement.py [-h] [-n RESOURCE_NAME]
                            [-f {DC_VOLTS,AC_VOLTS,DC_CURRENT,AC_CURRENT,_2_WIRE
_RES,_4_WIRE_RES,FREQ,PERIOD,TEMPERATURE,AC_VOLTS_DC_COUPLED,DIODE,WAVEFORM_VOLT
AGE,WAVEFORM_CURRENT,CAPACITANCE,INDUCTANCE}]
                            [-r RANGE] [-d DIGITS] [-op OPTION_STRING]
nidmm_measurement.py: error: unrecognized arguments: [pathtofile]\nidmm_measurement.py`

The definition of the `example` function contains the line:
`args = parser.parse_args(argsv)`

This line should be replaced with:
`args = parser.parse_args()`

With this substitution, the arguments are accepted, and if no arguments are supplied from the command prompt, the defined defaults are also used.  This leads to first try success on a example designed for getting started.
Updated version of this example has been attached as a .txt.
This has been validated against the Argparse Tutorial in the Python Documentation.

This has been the experience on Python 3.6.4, installed for all users on `C:\Program Files (x86)\Python36-32` on Windows 7 64-bit.

issue_title: Allure doesn't include information appended to Scenario or Feature description in before statements
issue_body:
#### I'm submitting a ... 
  - [ x] bug report
  - [ ] feature request

#### What is the current behavior?
If you include modify a Feature or Scenario description text in Behave's before_feature or before_scenario this is not reflected in the allure result.

#### Steps:
I've added something like this to my environment.py file 
```
def before_feature(context, feature):
    if 'server' in context.config.userdata:
        feature.name += ' on ' + context.config.userdata['server'] + ' environment.'

def before_scenario(context, scenario):
    if 'browser' in context.tags:
        context.driver = webdriver.Chrome()
        scenario.name += ' in ' + context.driver.capabilities['browserName'] + ' ' + context.driver.capabilities['version']
```
Then running with something like `behave -D server="testing" qa/functional/features/`

#### What is the expected behavior?
The feature should have ` on testing environment.` added to it on the allure report. And the scenario should have something like ` in chrome 68.0.3440.84` appended to the end of the name. Currently it includes the feature and scenario descriptions without the appended information. Behave will include this appended information if I do not run with the formatter. 

#### What is the motivation / use case for changing the behavior?
I'm trying to edit the Scenario and feature names without using Tables. 

#### Please tell us about your environment:
- Allure version:        2.6.0
- Test framework:     behave==1.2.6
- Allure adaptor:      allure-python==2.50

issue_title: Proposal: Client Go Rewrite
issue_body:
### Client Go Rewrite Outline

#### Packages

**main**: Does initial command line parsing and parses out the command, like `deisctl`
**client**: Parses command options and calls cmd package, like `deisctl`
**cmd**: takes options and works with models and api, somewhat like `deisctl`

**controller/client**:

- Orchestrates connection to controller, loads user preferences to configure a REST api used by `controller/models`. See [this comment](https://github.com/deis/deis/issues/3891#issuecomment-112996543).

**controller/models**:
 - high level api for working with the controller. Example of the `App` object:

```
Apps.Delete(app App) (error)
Apps.Get(id string) (App, error)
Apps.List()([]App, error)

type App struct {
  Id string
  Owner string
  Created time.Time
  Updated time.Time
  ...
}

(app) Save() (error)
```

##### Testing:

I plan to use a `httptest` server to mock the controller api.

issue_title: Add new feature: p-value of the Augmented Dickey-Fuller test
issue_body:
Currently we calculate the Augmented Dickey-Fuller test statistic as [feature](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh/blob/master/tsfresh/feature_extraction/feature_calculators.py#L253). The p-value of the Augmented Dickey-Fuller test might also be a relevant feature.

As the statsmodels function adfuller returns the test statistic as well as the p-value, it would make sense to memoize or buffer the results and add the new feature as a separate function.

A tutorial on how to add new features to tsfresh can be found in our documentation: http://tsfresh.readthedocs.io/en/latest/text/how_to_add_custom_feature.html

issue_title: Move `validate_seal` down to the VM class
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

The `validate_seal` method exists on the `Chain` class, however seal validation can and should be a `VM` level operation.

### How can it be fixed

Move the `validate_seal` method to the `VM` class.  The `Chain` class will need to call down to this method within it's `validate_block` method.

issue_title: Remove requirement files abstraction
issue_body:
By passing the `--with-requirements` flag, we try to retrieve requirement files (namely, `dev-requirements.txt` and `requirements.txt`) automatically from a pip installable archive or a local directory. While this might be nice for the user, it's not explicit and might confuse users. It is also not consistent what `pip` and `wheel` which require explicitly passing the relevant requirement files via multiple `-r` flags.

I suggest we remove that and allow users to pass multiple `-r` flags with explicit paths (local or URLs) to requirement files.

issue_title: python executable should not get symlinked from anaconda environment to package cache
issue_body:
We work on a central filesystem which does not allow hardlinks. We got failures for importing modules in environments which installed an updated python executable in the local package cache.

Following up, I discovered that the python executable in the newly created conda environment was installed by symlinking to the python in the package cache:

```
$ ls -l ~/.conda/envs/pytest/bin/python2.7
lrwxr-xr-x 1 feichtinger unx-ait 78 Jul  2 09:12 .conda/envs/pytest/bin/python2.7 -> .conda/envs/.pkgs/python-2.7.12-0/bin/python2.7
```

Since python dereferences argv0 by which it gets called, the sys.prefix which determines the module search path, ends up relative to the python in the package cache.

```
$ ~/.conda/envs/pytest/bin/python2.7 < <(echo 'import sys; print sys.prefix; print sys.exec_prefix')

~/.conda/envs/.pkgs/python-2.7.12-0
~/.conda/envs/.pkgs/python-2.7.12-0
```

But the sys.prefix should be relative to the environment, otherwise it cannot pick up the site-packages, etc. of the local environment. I think the python executable should not be installed via a symlink, but it should be copied to the conda environment location (same as is done in virtualenv).

Is there any solution already available besides copying the executable to the location manually? 

Many thanks,
Derek

issue_title: Using RequestsHttpConnection in 5.x code with proxies doesn't work
issue_body:
In our environment we use proxy servers. In addition we use AWS Elasticsearch domains hence the need to use the requests connection class with the custom auth.

We have found that proxies are not being honored in the 5.x code, and tracked it down to the change made in #481. Per the guidance issued by the requests authors in https://github.com/kennethreitz/requests/issues/3626, I believe the code should add a call to merge_environment_settings() in order to bring in the proxy settings. e.g. in perform_request() do:
  
```python
request = requests.Request(method=method, url=url, data=body)
prepared_request = self.session.prepare_request(request)
settings = self.session.merge_environment_settings(prepared_request.url, {}, None, None, None)
send_kwargs = {'timeout': timeout or self.timeout}
send_kwargs.update(settings)
response = self.session.send(prepared_request, **send_kwargs)
```

Another approach to take is to just go back to not using prepared requests. The reason I think you want the prepared_request is to be able to get to the `path_url` attr of the prepared request when an exception is raised (the path being used in trace logging). If we could get the path without needing to contruct an entire prepared_request then we can simplify the above down to one line again.

issue_title: Option to ignore warnings in connection classes
issue_body: I'm using my own SSL certificate, so I have `use_ssl=True` and `verify_certs=False`. This triggers a [warning](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/blob/master/elasticsearch/connection/http_requests.py#L57) (same if using `Urllib3HttpConnection`). Any objection to adding an `ignore_warnings` kwarg? Another option could be to have a `disable_warnings` function similar to that found in [urllib3](https://github.com/shazow/urllib3/blob/master/urllib3/__init__.py#L91).

issue_title: [Generic Update] we should show fields for objects that aren't set
issue_body: Today, we only show available fields on the object being mutated as options for the parameter path.  However, in the case of a VM without data disks, we give no hints as to which properties can be set--the user has to find a VM with a data disk and learn from that pattern.  We should find the SDK object that corresponds to the object (data disk in this case) and show available properties from that object.  This allows users to fill out empty fields without having to find a filled out example first.

issue_title: [App Service] Deployment slot list doesn't provide the actual "raw" slot name
issue_body:
If you run `az appservice web deployment slot swap -h`, you notice that you need to specify the name of the source deployment slot. However, if you run `az appservice web deployment list -ojsonc` in order to determine the value you can specify to the `swap` command, nowhere in that response do you actually get the "raw" slot name in the expected format. It responds with the following three properties:

1. `id` - The resource ID of the slot, in the format of `/subscriptions/<subscriptionId>/resourceGroups/<groupName>/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/<appName>/slots/<slotName>`

2. `name` - A string in the format of `<appName>/<slotName>`.

3. `siteName` - A string in the format of `<appName>(<slotName>)`

If you tried to specify the exact value of any of those three properties to the `swap` command, it will fail. They all include the `<slotName>`, however, you'd need to know to parse that substring out and use it when trying to swap. This seems needlessly complex, as opposed to the list simply displaying the slot name that actually matters, and is what the user would have specified when creating it in the first place.

The portal displays the list of slots with their name and their state (e.g. `Running`). It would be great if the CLI provided the same experience.

issue_title: Create a PR checklist to ensure completeness.
issue_body:
It would be nice to have a PR checklist for submitters in a similar vein to the checklist for Azure PowerShell (e.g., https://github.com/Azure/azure-powershell/pull/3466).

As a starting point, I would suggest, at a minimum:
* General:
    * Contribution guidelines.
* Tests:
    * Links to test information.
    * At least one unit test per command.
    * At least two popular scenario tests for each command.
* Documentation:
    * A meaningful description for each command added or updated.
    * A meaningful description for each parameter added or updated.
    * At least one meaningful example for each command added or updated.
* Anything else we think is a good idea to steal from Azure PowerShell.

issue_title: Unexplained CancelledErrors Over Extended Period
issue_body:
## Long story short

I am experiencing intermittent but frequent unexplained CancelledErrors under load in AIOHTTP2. I'm not sure if I am doing something wrong or their is an issue. Looking at server logs and metrics, I'm not seeing any evidence this is a downstream hangup on aiohttp. Within the aiohttp code, I catch the CancelledError and timed the aiohttp requests, and the cancellation is always immediate such as 0.001 seconds  (timex).

```
File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py", line 637, in __aenter__
    self._resp = yield from self._coro
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py", line 241, in _request
    yield from resp.start(conn, read_until_eof)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/client_reqrep.py", line 559, in start
    (message, payload) = yield from self._protocol.read()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/streams.py", line 509, in read
    yield from self._waiter
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/asyncio/futures.py", line 380, in __iter__
    yield self  # This tells Task to wait for completion.
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/asyncio/tasks.py", line 304, in _wakeup
    future.result()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/asyncio/futures.py", line 285, in result
    raise CancelledError
concurrent.futures._base.CancelledError
```

Reading through the source code, it looks like this is in trying to acquire a future. I'm not sure if i'm bumping into some sort of limit or something?

This is an issue for me as i'm consistently losing many requests due to this.

## Expected behaviour

CancelledErrors when valid to not be so immediate or frequent, and to be the result of a downstream timeout or similar.

## Actual behaviour

Seeing sporadic cancelled errors, but consistently occurring over periods of multiple hours, all in the same place inside aiohttp of trying to acquire a future, and always immediate.

## Steps to reproduce

Running many requests though an aiohttp server over time. ClientSession is shared across all requests. I currently have timeouts set as `ClientSession(loop=app.loop, read_timeout=120, conn_timeout=30)`

## Your environment

aiohttp 2.2.0, ubuntu, python 3.5,

issue_title: Convert all nbgrader subcommands to standalone objects
issue_body: If we want to run `nbgrader assign`, `nbgrader autograde`, and `nbgrader feedback` from the notebook web app (see #555), we will also have to create standalone objects that can perform these operations without inheriting from `JupyterApp` (as was done with `nbgrader release`, `fetch`, `collect`, etc.) or shell out to the command line to run these commands. The former approach is more work, but in the long run much better I think. The reason why shelling out would be necessary is that both the notebook and nbgrader inherit from `JupyterApp`, which is a singleton, and thus both can't be instantiated at the same time.

issue_title: Dies too soon with cmake missing
issue_body:
skbuild fails to import if cmake is missing. This means that the following code will not work (untested, so it might not work as is, too):

```python
import subprocess

# Add CMake as a build requirement if cmake is not installed or is too low a version
setup_requires = []
try:
    cmake_version = subprocess.check_output(["cmake", "--version"])
    cmake_version = [int(x) for x in cmake_version.splitlines()[0].split()[-1].split('.')]
    if cmake_version < (3,4):
        setup_requires.append('cmake')
except OSError:
    setup_requires.append('cmake')
```

This forces the addition of `cmake` to the `pyproject.toml`, which could install an older version of cmake (3.10 instead of 3.11), and could affect the user (cmake from python might be installed in the path). If scikit build didn't fail until setup was run (and `setup_requires` was run), then the code at the beginning would be less invasive.

Note: testing code like this is easy with:

```bash
docker run -it ubuntu
apt update && apt install g++ python-pip python-dev git ninja-build
pip install pip==10.0b1
# get a scikit-build project and cd to it
python -m pip install .
```

Due to what I consider a bug in pip 10, you have to use `python -m pip` instead of `pip`.

issue_title: Accept pluralized fields from the server
issue_body:
Entities may have one to many relationships with one an other, and the names used to describe these fields can vary. For example, the `Organization` entity has a one to many relationship with the `Media` and `Subnet` entities via the "media" and "subnet" fields. An HTTP GET request to `/katello/api/v2/organizations/:id` returns a JSON payload like this:

```json
{
    'media': [{'id': 1}, {'id': 2}],
    'subnets': [{'id': 3}, {'id': 4}],
    …
}
```

Similarly, the `Location` entity has a one to many relationship with the `SmartProxy` entity via the "smart_proxy" field. An HTTP GET request to `/api/v2/locations/:id` returns a JSON payload like this:

```json
{
    'smart_proxies': [{'id': 3}, {'id': 4}],
    …
}
```

In the above example, there are two different spellings for a field:

* The field-name as-is.
* A pluralized version of the field name.

And as described in #110, a one to many relationship may be described with a name in the form `<field_name>_ids`.

NailGun should be capable of dealing with the pluralized version of a field name.

issue_title: .NET Core 2.0: ResourceManager always reads neutral RESX file
issue_body:
# General

VS2017, 15.3.5

I have an ASP.NET Core 2.0 app to which I added two resource files - the neutral one and one for German:

* SiteResources.resx (English strings)
* SiteResources.de.resx (German strings)

Both have their Build Action set to `EmbeddedResource`  and the `ResxFileGenerator` as the Custom Tool. So far so good.

The problem is: The following code returns always the values from the neutral RESX file:

    SiteResources.Culture = new CultureInfo("de");
    string s1 = SiteResources.SomeTestString;

Same would go for this, which is equivalent

    string s1 = SiteResources.ResourceManager.GetString("SomeTestString", new CultureInfo("de"));

This somewhat defeats the whole purpose of having Resx files and the designer. Any idea how to work around this?

issue_title: Memory usage by dateutil.zoneinfo increased significantly from 2.2 to 2.3
issue_body:
From change log of version 2.3: 
- Changed many aspects of dealing with the zone info file. Instead of a cache,
  all the zones are loaded to memory, but symbolic links are loaded only once,
  so not much memory is used.

As of 2.3 zoneinfo loads all the timezones from tar file in memory once and keeps it there.
I used this script to check the implication of this:
```python
#!/usr/bin/env python
import resource
import gc

def memory_usage_mb():
    return float(resource.getrusage(resource.RUSAGE_SELF).ru_maxrss) / (1024 * 1024)

mem1 = memory_usage_mb()
print "Memory used before zoneinfo.gettz(): {0:.2f}Mb".format(mem1)
from dateutil import zoneinfo
zoneinfo.gettz('America/Los_Angeles')
gc.collect()
mem2 = memory_usage_mb()
print "Memory used after zoneinfo.gettz(): {0:.2f}Mb".format(mem2)
print "Delta: {0:.2f}Mb".format(mem2 - mem1)
```
shell output
```shell
$ git co 2.1
$ ./test_memory
Memory used before zoneinfo.gettz(): 3.86Mb
Memory used after zoneinfo.gettz(): 6.10Mb
Delta: 2.24Mb

$ git co 2.4.2
$ ./test_memory
Memory used before zoneinfo.gettz(): 3.83Mb
Memory used after zoneinfo.gettz(): 10.60Mb
Delta: 6.77Mb
```

Probably reason behind this was to save time on reading from tar file, which maybe very practical if in one script `zoneinfo.gettz()` is used often.

On the other hand, if in web environment one web worker maybe running multiple processes and adding memory overhead even 5Mb may affect the max number of processes that can be handled by 1 web worker, especially if system timezones are sufficient and `zoneinfo.gettz()` is not being used often.

Maybe as a compromise an additional config value can be added to specify the behavior.

issue_title: Release 2.5.0
issue_body:
I've been really negligent in getting this release out, I think it's time to wrap up whatever work needs to be done and get a release out ASAP. I'm creating this issue to discuss exactly what should be included in 2.5.0 and what should be pushed to later releases.

**2.5.0**:
-  tzwin fixes:
    - PR #142
    - Issue #143, #148, #144
- tz fixes:
    - Issue #132 
- RRule
    - Move `smart_defaults` back into a feature branch (#30) - in the time since adding this, I've been thinking we may want to implement this differently, and I don't want to get stuck maintaining API compatibility. (This should automatically fix #126 for the release, but that should also be updated on the feature branch). I think this should be re-implemented with a combination of #125 and breaking up `parse` into smaller, customizable functions.
    - PR #136 (performance enhancement)
    - Issue #145 - This is apparently a big problem for at least one project, so we don't want it to persist through two releases.
- Parser:
    - PR #130 (properly handle dates prior to year 100)
    - Issue #78 (raise error on empty string)
- Relativedelta:
    - Issue #40 - This is mostly implemented, I'll clean up and make PR.
- build:
    - Either fix `updatezinfo` so that it doesn't fail so much or add some fallback logic to the build.

Things to push to 2.5.1:
    - Issue #70 (API changes - don't want to rush)
    - Issue #79 (I have this half-done, but it's a bit complicated to do so I need to add tests and I don't want to put things off)

@jarondl Anything critical I'm missing?

issue_title: setup.py fails on Python3.4 (PEP 448)
issue_body:
Running `python3 setup.py gen_tl` on the latest commit results in `Failed to import TelegramClient due to can use starred expression only as assignment target (__init__.py, line 286)`

This was with Python3.4, while Python3.5 works just fine. Telethon 0.13.6 (0f10e14) works fine as well. The actual file/line is `telethon/tl/types/__init__.py`:

    return b''.join((b':\x1d\xe8\x98',struct.pack('<i', self.user_id),TLObject.serialize_string(self.description),b'\x15\xc4\xb5\x1c',struct.pack('<i', len(self.commands)),*[(x.to_bytes()) for x in self.commands],))

And if I researched correctly, then the issue is that [PEP 448](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0448/) is not supported in Python3.4.

issue_title: send_read_acknowledge for supergroup locks the update thread
issue_body:
So I have a class that implements `TelegramClient` and a method with this code. I know that supergroups are not regular chats, but then there is this strange behavior.

```
print("Gets printed")
self.send_read_acknowledge(self.chats[self.supergroup], [update.message])
print("Never gets printed")
```

`self.chats` is a dictionary of entities based on their id. The third line actually never gets printed if I send the request this way. I have tried sending `ReadHistoryRequest`, `ReadMessageContentsRequest` and something else: the line gets printed then, but the unread messages counter never sets to zero.

The only successful way of removing the counter is to send a message to the supergroup (which is a thing of Telegram, I guess). Checked only with the web version of the client launched in a separate window.

I am really sorry if this has already been brought up, but so far I only found the issues for channels, not supergroups.

Thanks!

issue_title: Parse views docstrings for summary/description
issue_body:
Following up on a discussion in flask-rest-api: https://github.com/Nobatek/flask-rest-api/pull/3.

A contributor suggested to parse the docstrings for `summary`. I think we can go further and also get `description`:

```py
def my_view(...):
"""Summary

Long description...
"""
```

This could be done in apispec and it is compatible with the YAML docstring parsing:

```py
def my_view(...):
"""Summary

Long description...
---
YAML stuff
"""
```

I didn't take much time to look into it any deeper.

Marking as 1.0 as I would like to at least take needed utils out of yaml_utils.py (`trim_docstring`, maybe `dedent`) back into utils (those have no yaml dependency).

Marking as backward incompatible in case the feature is activated by default.

issue_title: post_dump is passing a list of objects as original object
issue_body:
Hi,

I think post_dump with pass_original=True should pass the original object related to the data serialized and not a list of objects which this object belongs to.

``` python
from marshmallow import fields, post_dump, Schema

class DeviceSchema(Schema):
    id = fields.String()

    @post_dump(pass_original=True)
    def __post_dump(self, data, obj):
        print(obj)  # <-- this is a list

devices = [dict(id=1), dict(id=2)]
DeviceSchema().dump(devices, many=True)
```

In the above example, the parameter `obj` is a list of devices rather than the device object itself.

What do you think?

issue_title: Proofs without Abbreviation
issue_body: Z3's proofs contain many subformula abbreviations, through let statements that introduce new names of the form "$x..." . While this has the advantage of making the proof more compact, it also has the disadvantage of making the proof harder for humans to read. Is there an option to disable these abbreviations in the proof production? If not, could you implement it?

issue_title: How to translate the Expr(Const number) in Z3 to be an object in MPQ/MPFR
issue_body:
When I use the Z3-opt(Ocaml version) to solve some planning problem, and I get a result from Z3 like this:

(+ (/ 31.0 10.0) (* (to_real (- 1)) epsilon))

And this result is of type of Z3.Expr. I want to know how to translate this result(of Z3.Expr) to anther common data-type object(for example, MPQ/MPFR).Does the Z3-opt(Ocaml version) provide the ocaml interface to this?
The result like (+ (/ 31.0 10.0) (* (to_real (- 1)) epsilon)) is to complex. 
I could not use this in my project. How could I get the rational number it represents?

Many thanks.

issue_title: c++ API, func_decl function
issue_body:
Dear All,

I would like to encode an uninterpreted function with a number of parameters using C++ AP. I was going to use 
`
func_decl function(char const * name, sort_vector const& domain, sort const& range);`

However, it looks like this function is not defined in z3++.h (please see lines 2660--2682).
I can use a similar function to obtain the same result, i.e. `func_decl context::function(..,sort_vector const& domain,..)`.
I was wondering if there is a reason why` func_decl function(..,sort_vector const& domain,..)` should not be used (and, perhaps, 
`func_decl context::function(..,sort_vector const& domain,..)` should not be used as well).

Thank you!

nina

issue_title: Typo in installation instructions
issue_body:
The instructions at docs/user/installation.rst contain the instruction:

    python -c "import theano; print theano.sandbox.cuda.dnn.dnn_available() or theano.sandbox.cuda.dnn.dnn_avalailable.msg"

Should the second "avalailable" be spelt "available"?
If I run it as written I get a Python error message, while if I run it with the normal spelling it tells me that the library is not supported by my card.

(It may be worth noting at this point in the instructions that compute capability 3.0 is required - and that you can determine which cards support this by looking at the list here: https://developer.nvidia.com/cuda-gpus )

issue_title: Append git hash to version when installing from git
issue_body: Given the infrequency of pypi releases many users install Lasgne from git rather than from pypi. Because of this, the version string does not full capture the installed version (i.e. the git hash). I would suggest adding the commit hash to the version string when installing from git. This would be consistent with [what Theano does](https://github.com/Theano/Theano/blob/149d008d528277988867089cb607105e706910c4/setup.py#L134-L143).

issue_title: Will JupyterLab be supported?
issue_body:
Out of the box, ipyvega can't draw plots in JupyterLab because of this tightened security measure:

```
JavaScript output is disabled in JupyterLab
```

Some libraries, like Altair, have [managed to find a way around it](https://github.com/altair-viz/altair/issues/659). Are there any plans to do the same for ipyvega?

issue_title: Interactive plotting cuts off figures
issue_body:
E.g. see this image:

issue_title: *** AttributeError: 'Money' object has no attribute 'value'
issue_body:
django==1.11.7
djangorestframework==3.7.3
py-moneyed==0.7.0         # via django-money
django-money==0.12


**models.py**
```python
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from django.contrib.contenttypes.fields import GenericRelation
from django.db import models
from djmoney.models.fields import MoneyField

from poinkbackend.apps.activities.models import Activity, ActivityMixin
from poinkbackend.apps.branches.models import Branch
from poinkbackend.apps.commons.models import AbstractTimestamp


class Order(ActivityMixin, AbstractTimestamp):
    poink = models.PositiveIntegerField()
    amount = MoneyField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, default_currency='THB')
    cashier = models.ForeignKey(User, related_name='orders', on_delete=models.PROTECT)
    branch = models.ForeignKey(Branch, related_name='orders', on_delete=models.PROTECT, null=True, blank=True)

    activities = GenericRelation(Activity, related_query_name='orders', related_name='orders')

    def __str__(self):
        return f'{self.amount.value}'
```

Another one
```python
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from django.contrib.contenttypes.fields import GenericForeignKey
from django.contrib.contenttypes.models import ContentType
from django.db import models
from djchoices import DjangoChoices, ChoiceItem
from djmoney.forms import MoneyField

from poinkbackend.apps.commons.models import AbstractTimestamp


class ActivityMixin:
    @property
    def get_activity(self):
        ctype = ContentType.objects.get_for_model(self.__class__)
        try:
            event = Activity.objects.get(content_type__pk=ctype.id, object_id=self.id)
        except:
            return None
        return event


class Activity(AbstractTimestamp):
    """
    Poink Transaction. I name activities to avoid confusion between Django transaction
    type will dictates the sign +/-
    """

    class ActivityType(DjangoChoices):
        redeem = ChoiceItem("redeem")
        order = ChoiceItem("order")
        amend = ChoiceItem("amend")

    type = models.CharField(max_length=10, choices=ActivityType.choices, default=ActivityType.order, db_index=True)
    note = models.TextField(null=True, blank=True)  # Amend need this
    amount = MoneyField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, default_currency='THB')
    balance = MoneyField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, default_currency='THB')
    customer = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.PROTECT)

    # GenericRelation
    content_type = models.ForeignKey(ContentType, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    object_id = models.PositiveIntegerField()
    content_object = GenericForeignKey()

    def __str__(self):
        return f'{self.type} | {self.amount} | {self.balance} | {self.customer} | {self.created_at}'

    class Meta:
        unique_together = ('content_type', 'object_id')
        permissions = (
            ("view_activity", "View Activity"),
        )
```

issue_title: Internationalization of text
issue_body:
![screenshot 2016-02-05 14 00 26](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/836375/12857618/d3c18396-cc10-11e5-9833-f9c3ae8c8a96.png)

As we progress, we should take some time to think about how we do internationalization of text. The current way that people appear to be doing this in React apps is with [react-intl](https://github.com/yahoo/react-intl), which relies on [formatjs](http://formatjs.io/). There are standard APIs for the easy ones:

```
> new Intl.NumberFormat().format(1000.0)
"1,000"
```

It's the string messages that I'm not sure of. What I do know we have to do is make it easy for others to provide translations as PRs. Seems like people are used to JSON files with things like templated dialog messages, etc. for all the languages. @twistedhardware suggested that there may be translations available for a lot of common things like `File`, `Edit`, etc.

Within jupyter/notebook, @twistedhardware is putting together an enhancement proposal for the current Tornado app and templates. We should make sure to learn from both efforts.
## Related resources
- [formatjs](http://formatjs.io/)
- [react-intl](https://github.com/yahoo/react-intl)
- [Video on Formatting with Format.js + react-intl](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Sla-DkvmIHY)

issue_title: Request kernel shutdown

issue_body:

```
from django.db import models
from django.utils import timezone
from wagtail.wagtailcore.models import Page, PageManager, PageQuerySet

class EventPageQuerySet(PageQuerySet):
    def future(self):
        today = timezone.localtime(timezone.now()).date()
        return self.filter(start_date__gte=today)

class EventPage(Page):
    start_date = models.DateField()

    objects = PageManager.from_queryset(EventPageQuerySet)()

issue_title: Can I build an IRSB from a combination of instructions and IR?
issue_body:
So, I am running instructions, and between instructions I want to set some registers & memory locations to fix values - these are known before generation of the IRSB.

I know I can use the breakpoints to do what I want, but I was hoping to do it without breakpoints - mainly because I don't want to keep track of where in the execution I am - but also because I hope to gain speed.

I could probably add extra instructions, but this would require me to also modify the instruction pointer after each added instruction - probably creating more problems. Also, it would make me bound to a specific instruction set.

So, my hope is to, given a list of normal instructions in binary format, and some points where I want to change register data, build a list of normal instructions and single registry/memory modifying IR instructions that can be used to build one IRSB.

Is this possible and how?

issue_title: Challenge the relevance of Redis backends in core
issue_body:
My opinion is that having Redis in the main Kinto repo, and not in a separate repo:

* is mainly an intellectual exercise to demostrate that the backend interface is agnostic
* introduces a overhead of maintenance (redis methods to port, redis server to run the tests, redis on travis)
* has happened because Remy and Alex are huge fans of Redis :)

Whereas concretely, the Redis has a number of limitations that prevents its usage in production:

* there is no «per-request» transaction
* there is no schema migration
* the filtering and sorting is done in memory in python, after deserializing each records from json strings
* [some methods are implemented naively](https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/blob/3.2.1/kinto/core/storage/memory.py#L31-L40)

Using the redis backend for cache and postgres for storage and permissions can be relevant, but the absence of transaction (two-phase commit) prevents redis to be used along postgres for storage or permission backends.

Of course we could spend ~~some~~ a lot of time improving all this, but we don't have any use-case and it looks like the few production deployments we've heard of (mozilla, kinto-heroku etc.) are using postgresql. So we could simply move the redis backend to a separate repo :)

**What do you think ?**

![](http://i.giphy.com/Zu2mUFvWg37kA.gif)

issue_title: Spikes, twisted edges and waves in pointcloud [F200]
issue_body:
+ Partially reflective materials cause spikes (peaks and valleys) in resulting pointcloud.
+ Flat surfaces are often projected with wavy/dancing points (varies with distance from camera) and even more often the same flat surfaces have twisted (upwards) edges.

Example (average distance from camera ~30cm):
![pc_example_5_annotated](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/14088269/12959149/7ec48ae0-d035-11e5-826b-c385582bb974.png)
![pc_example_6_annotated](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/14088269/12959155/88a71c44-d035-11e5-95f6-d0dfec3a5560.png)
![config_example_annotated](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/14088269/12959145/77258820-d035-11e5-9a64-4e634483368b.png)

By twiddling and adjusting all parameters, best results for getting rid of the spikes gave drastic reduction of the laser power which also reduces the pointcloud size, and effectively renders it useless (even then the spikes and twisted edges are not completely eliminated).

Is this an expected behavior or something worthy to resolve?

Another example of twisted edges (37cm average distance):
![2pc_example_annotated](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/14088269/12959230/dd8aa50a-d035-11e5-8019-1c25a6399b44.png)

Note: Screenshots are made after camera was tested and running (non-continuously) for several hours; I observed better average results than in the first ~hour after a cold start.

PS: While running `cpp-config-ui` I ran into TemperatureControlLoop error (#26 but only on one camera; also had it couple of times when running `cpp-tutorial-3-pointcloud`) and one-time irreproducible `timed_mutex::try_lock_for(...) timed out` exception which didn't freeze neither view.

My idea would be to have stuff like _route_,min_rf, etc in kwargs, that then can be included in the "params" right before making a request. Makes sense ?

And if there are kwargs-keys that are needed on other parts (ex: a router may need a "prefer_leader", it can pop it from the kwargs) ?

issue_title: Forgetting variables in CONSTANCE_CONFIG_FIELDSETS makes the admin form unusable
issue_body:
### Describe the problem

If one forgets a variable in `CONSTANCE_CONFIG_FIELDSETS`, the constance admin interface fails to update the other fields with new values. After clicking on "Save", the values displayed just afterwards in the form are still the new ones, but they have not been saved on the database (my settings, but I guess it's the same with Redis), and the flag "modified" is not updated. 

The reason is [that the form is not valid](https://github.com/jazzband/django-constance/blob/484ab4113293349f880c70b964282a2dd6b3eae2/constance/admin.py#L204) because there is then a required filed is missing.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Have two constance variable, say `FOO`  and  `BAR`: 
```
CONSTANCE_CONFIG = {
    'FOO': (42, 'Answer to the Ultimate Question'),
    'BAR': (47, 'Answer to the Ultimate Question, taking into account Inflation'),
}
```` 
2. Forget `BAR` in `CONSTANCE_CONFIG_FIELDSETS`
```
CONSTANCE_CONFIG_FIELDSETS = {
    'Answers': ('FOO'),
}
```
3. Go to the constance admin page, and change the value of `FOO`. The new value is not saved, because the form is not valid because the field `BAR` is missing.

### Suggestion

Raise an error? Let me a couple of weeks and I'll have the time to do a PR

### System configuration

* Django version:  1.10.2
* Python version: 2.7.9

issue_title: [question] otLinkSetExtendedAddress and IEEE EUI-64
issue_body:
Hi,

Thread specification explicitly prohibits the use of a factory assigned IEEE EUI-64 (Table 3-2). The only exception is out-of-band commissioning when the extended address MAY be set to IEEE EUI-64, however given the nature of "out-of-band" it is not going to be used anyway.

So more out of curiosity, rather than technical reason, I have few questions:
1) If IEEE EUI-64 is forced as an extended address during commissioning or normal operation, what kind of issues can it cause? Can it compromise network security?
2) What if IEEE EUI-64 is exposed to public by other means, but is not used directly as a an extended address?
3) Given the fact, that OpenThread takes care of generating random addresses, is there any use case where otLinkSetExtendedAddress should be used?

issue_title: Handle CSV input other than UTF-8 e.g. ANSI
issue_body:
I am using Flask-Excel with the first example in the docs, and the first line fails, unless the CSV is in UTF-8 e.g. I have a customer with an ANSI file and I want to be able to import it. The line is as follows, and is therefore just hitting pyexcel (hence raising this here):

`jsonify({"result": request.get_array(field_name='file')})`

The trace is as follows:

`  File "/code/app/myfile.py", line 99, in upload_file
    if request.method == 'POST':
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyexcel_webio/__init__.py", line 81, in get_array
    return pe.get_array(**params)	
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyexcel/core.py", line 290, in get_array
    sheet = get_sheet(**keywords)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyexcel/core.py", line 68, in get_sheet
    sheet = Sheet(named_content.payload, named_content.name, **sheet_params)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyexcel/sheets/sheet.py", line 87, in __init__
    transpose_after=transpose_after
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyexcel/sheets/sheet.py", line 130, in init
    Matrix.__init__(self, sheet)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyexcel/sheets/matrix.py", line 36, in __init__
    self.__width, self.__array = uniform(list(array))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyexcel_io/sheet.py", line 55, in to_array
    for row_index, row in enumerate(self.row_iterator()):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyexcel_io/_compact.py", line 43, in next
    return type(self).__next__(self)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pyexcel_io/fileformat/_csv.py", line 42, in __next__
    return next(self.reader).encode('utf-8')
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/codecs.py", line 630, in next
    line = self.readline()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/codecs.py", line 545, in readline
    data = self.read(readsize, firstline=True)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/codecs.py", line 492, in read
    newchars, decodedbytes = self.decode(data, self.errors)
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf8' codec can't decode byte 0xca in position 0: invalid continuation byte`

Going through the code, while _csv.py supports the notion of different encodings (e.g. UTF8Recorder has a reader and it supplies the encoding) unless I am mistaken, the encoding is determined by the Python version in _compact.py (otherwise through system default encoding), rather than it being possible to supply it as a parameter to get_array() that cascades down.

issue_title: Remote execution fails when local_repo is too big
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

When the directory specified by the local_repo dir is too big, remote execution fails with the confusing message "taskflow.exceptions.StorageFailure: Storage backend internal error". This is a frequent error case if the directory pointed to is actually a git repo (i.e., also contains associated git metadata).

### Expected behavior

Artman should validate that the size of local_repo is < 1 MB.

### Action

Accumulate the size of local_repo here: https://github.com/googleapis/artman/blob/e83b08313c7fff2cb1f5ca6f716220050d1d4fd9/execute_pipeline.py#L221
and throw a meaningful exception if it is too big.

issue_title: Assertion "counts.size"
issue_body:
This error is similar to the one described in #73 using the last version of FastText.

The error message is:
`assertion "counts.size() == osz_" failed: file "src/model.cc", line 231, function: void fasttext::Model::setTargetCounts(const std::vector<long int>&)`

Although in #73 it was discussed that the problem was that the model is not loaded correctly, in my case it is because is not saved correctly during the supervised training.

More specifically, I used the parameters: dim=100, epoch=10, lr=0.0514, minCount=5, wordNgrams=2.  The .bin file is about 1.5Mb, while the .vec file is around 260Mb.

If I train the same model on an older version of fastText, the .bin file is 2.58 Gb, while the .vec is around 260Mb.

Using other parameters, like (dim=100, epoch=5, lr=0.2283, minCount=1, wordNgrams=5) over the same training data, creates correctly the model.

FastText was compiled with gcc 6.4.0 over Cygwin.

I'll test the same version of FastText on Linux this afternoon.

issue_title: Support multiple owners_after and different from owner_before, for the CREATE
issue_body:
current `BigchainDB.transactions.create()`:

```python    
def create(self, payload=None, verifying_key=None, signing_key=None):
    # ...
```
assumes that the verifying_key represents both the owner before and the owner after. This is one possible case, but there are others, which we also need to support. The other cases are that there can be one owner before, and multiple owners after, which differ from the owner before.

issue_title: Switch to rich-text CHANGELOG, like agate has
history and list commands are largely redundant
issue_body:
The **history** and **list** commands largely have the same basic functionality of displaying commands from the history. 

There is a partial overlap in the functionality where they both support some of the same features.  But each also has at least one feature that the other doesn't have.

It would probably be more convenient for end users to combine all of the functionality into one common **history** command and remove the **list** command.

```
	mloader.def("saveGroup", [](const std::string& filename, std::unique_ptr<Group>& ptr)
	{
		std::ofstream os(filename, std::ios::binary);
		cereal::PortableBinaryOutputArchive archive(os);
	        // std::unique_ptr<Group> ptr;
		archive(ptr);
    });
```

_Compiles_
```
	mloader.def("saveGroup", [](const std::string& filename/*, std::unique_ptr<Group>& ptr*/)
	{
		std::ofstream os(filename, std::ios::binary);
		cereal::PortableBinaryOutputArchive archive(os);
	    std::unique_ptr<Group> ptr;
		archive(ptr);
    });
```

issue_title: please consider adding (n,m) constructors
issue_body:
I believe a useful enhancement would be constructors of the form:

array_t<double> a (1,2)

to create an empty, 2-d array of dim 1x2

This would make the library interface more like many other array libraries, and ease porting.

Unfortunately, my modern c++ foo is not quite up to this.  The existing array_t has a constructor of the form
array_t<double> a (std::vector<size_t>)
So a simple approach is to transform a variable arg list into a std::vector and use the existing constructor.

BTW, I think it's strange the only constructor (of an empty array) takes a very specific std::vector rather than some more generic sequence, or am I misreading the code?

issue_title: Remove optional dependencies
issue_body:
I think we should consider this for our next release as it will dramatically simplify our code. If we remove `already_listening` as #3623 suggests, our optional dependencies have little to no benefit.

The biggest change this would cause is the `simple_verify` method in ACME for the DNS challenge either needs to:
1. Raise `NotImplementedError`
2. Always return `True`.

If any third party plugins/clients that use ACME use this method, we should probably just return `True`. Otherwise, we should probably do the former and consider removing the `simple_verify` method from all challenge objects.

issue_title: Certbot docs don't build in Sphinx 1.6.1+
issue_labels: [{'id': 245387015, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwyNDUzODcwMTU=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20documentation', 'name': 'area: documentation', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 281347824, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwyODEzNDc4MjQ=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/has%20pr', 'name': 'has pr', 'color': '0052cc', 'default': False}, {'id': 175439992, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWwxNzU0Mzk5OTI=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/low%20priority', 'name': 'low priority', 'color': 'bfe5bf', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
In their 1.6.1 release, Sphinx started requiring the package `latexmk` when trying to make PDFs of docs using the Makefile target `latexpdf`. This would be fine, but it also came with a change in behavior for us.

Apparently, Sphinx does not support SVGs when building PDFs (see sphinx-doc/sphinx#1907). Previously, this would be silent error and our documentation would just build without the SVG images. Now it's a hard error and causes the build to fail.

While it's nice that we're now aware of the problem, it has broken our tests on the https://github.com/certbot/website repo. I'm going to pin `Sphinx<=1.5.6` until this issue has either been resolved by us or upstream. This problem only affects the Certbot package and the problem is the badges in the Certbot README.

issue_title: Certbot to update not only nginx.conf but config in sites-enabled
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):
CentOS 7

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
OS package manager (yum)

## I ran this command and it produced this output:
certbot --nginx

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:
Upgrade config files in /etc/nginx/sites-enabled folder as well

So my question is basically how can I tell certbot where to look for an nginx config to upgrade? Do I have to always copy all my server blocks into nginx.conf, upgrade with certbot, then copy the modifications back to my sites-enabled config file?

issue_title: Report malformed response before missing nonce
issue_body: In our testing, sometimes Akamai fails to reach Let's Encrypt, and returns a 500 with malformed (non-JSON) contents. Certbot reports this as a missing nonce; instead it should report the malformed body and ignore the lack of a nonce.

issue_title: Cerbot generated files better explained
issue_body:
It would be nice if the setup docs would explain the generated pem files....
Running the ./certbot-auto certonly, generates the required certificates but they are confusing since most configs refer to them as *.key, *.cert etc...

chain.pem  => ca file (*.ca)

privkey.pem => key file (*.key)

I do get that these are "just'" text files, but this was a friction point for me.

Chris

https://certbot.eff.org/#debianwheezy-nginx

issue_title: Modify unique_id(UUID) format.
issue_body:
@Basha-Shaik 

* Last update of basescript code we implemented `unique_id` construction in this format `"20171214T203104_b4141665-e10d-11e7-8fe1-901b0ecdc716"`
* In our `crawler` components for `clusterevent` logs we are creating `unique_id` by taking `event_id` in this format `"20171214T203104_b4141667e10d67e78fe1901b0ecdc716"`
* Finally the `unique_id` for logs in two different formats difference is `-`.
* We discussed with @prashanthellina about this issue so we are decided to convert at `basescript` code remove `-` in between id using `uuid.uuid1().hex` to maintain one structure.

issue_title: Add ability to write YAML blocks based on test decorator
issue_body:
As of tap version 13 YAML blocks are supported. Tappy allows for reading these YAML blocks but it would be great if the pytest tap plugin could write the YAML blocks based on some information coming from the tests themselves.

I implemented this locally using a decorator for the tests and by adding an extra hook method to the pytest tap plugin.py. 

The decorator takes a string which represents the body of the YAML block which should be added to the tap report and adds it to the TestCase in question,

The hook method then takes this additional information and adds it to the TAP line to be written. 

In this way the YAML block is defined by the test writer but it might be nice to be able to fill the YAML block with some of the other automated outputs of tests.

I've got some code I can share but new to this area so not sure if it's the right way to do it. Links very closely to an issue I just submitted [here](https://github.com/python-tap/tappy/issues/78)

issue_title: Only have one place for Raiden specification
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Currently two versions of Raiden specification are available.
https://raiden-network.readthedocs.io/en/stable/spec.html# and
https://raiden-network-specification.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html

The two specs differ widely and it seems that https://raiden-network.readthedocs.io/en/stable/spec.html# is not really being maintained. 

It is also confusing that the spec that is to be found on the official read the docs, points to the outdated version.

## Suggested solutions
The way I see it we have two options. 

1. Remove the outdated one entirely and links to the maintained one in the official read the docs (easy solution). Questions is if we want to keep parts of the "old spec" or if it can be removed entirely.

2. Merge the two specs into one. This would require some work to get rid of duplicated content and updating the "old spec".

## Tasks
- [ ] Decide on one of the above solutions
- [ ] Implement decided solution

issue_title: Wrong url inferred for BambooHR OpenID Connect
issue_body:
I made a custom backend for BambooHR OpenID Connect that looks like this:
```python
from social_core.backends.open_id_connect import OpenIdConnectAuth
class BambooHROpenIdConnectAuth(OpenIdConnectAuth):
    name = 'bamboohr-oidc'
    @property
    def OIDC_ENDPOINT(self):
        return 'https://7geese.bamboohr.com'
```

The BambooHR OIDC config (e.g. https://7geese.bamboohr.com/.well-known/openid-configuration) specifies the `authorization_endpoint` as https://7geese.bamboohr.com/authorize.php?request=authorize. However, the code to generate the final authorization url here: https://github.com/python-social-auth/social-core/blob/2854a7e1d518f83662b7ad480ffe3ff069c02691/social_core/backends/oauth.py#L343
does not expect the authorization endpoint to already contain a query string and blindly adds another `?` before appending all other url parameters, resulting in an url that starts with something like https://7geese.bamboohr.com/authorize.php?request=authorize?nonce=..., i.e. two query strings?!

I was able to fix my specific issue by applying this patch:

```diff
diff --git a/social_core/backends/oauth.py b/social_core/backends/oauth.py
index 9f39842..a795636 100644
--- a/social_core/backends/oauth.py
+++ b/social_core/backends/oauth.py
@@ -335,12 +335,7 @@ class BaseOAuth2(OAuthAuth):
         params = self.auth_params(state)
         params.update(self.get_scope_argument())
         params.update(self.auth_extra_arguments())
-        params = urlencode(params)
-        if not self.REDIRECT_STATE:
-            # redirect_uri matching is strictly enforced, so match the
-            # providers value exactly.
-            params = unquote(params)
-        return '{0}?{1}'.format(self.authorization_url(), params)
+        return url_add_parameters(self.authorization_url(), params)
 
     def auth_complete_params(self, state=None):
         client_id, client_secret = self.get_key_and_secret()
```

but I assume throwing out the whole logic around the `REDIRECT_STATE` here can't be the right way to go. But I can't make sense of that logic: Why is the `params` string unquoted? Couldn't that lead to invalid urls if any of the params contain e.g. any of `?`, `#`, `&`?

A simpler fix could be to check if `?` is already included in `self.authorization_url()` and then use an `&` instead.

Happy to open a PR with a fix, but I feel I should understand the code around it first :-)

issue_title: [build system] hanging docker containers after build fails or aborts
issue_body:
### System information
- **Linux Ubuntu 16.04**:
- **Ray installed from source for CI builds**:

### Describe the problem
When a Ray build ends prematurely, and after test/jenkins_tests/multi_node_docker_test.py runs, it leaves behind docker containers that take ~500G of disk space.

### Source code / logs
in test/jenkins_tests/multi_node_docker_test.py:
```
        command = (["docker", "run", "-d"] + mem_arg + shm_arg + volume_arg +
                   [docker_image, "ray", "start", "--head", "--block",
                    "--redis-port=6379",
                    "--num-redis-shards={}".format(num_redis_shards),
                    "--num-cpus={}".format(num_cpus),
                    "--num-gpus={}".format(num_gpus),
                    "--no-ui"])
        print("Starting head node with command:{}".format(command))

        proc = subprocess.Popen(command,
                                stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE)
```

after speaking w/dan crankshaw about his docker testing approach, he strongly suggests the following (which i agree with):

1.  add a tag/label to each and every docker container that is launched
2.  add a final step to your tests that goes through this list of tags and explicitly kills these containers

issue_title: Passing Files breaks over IPP
issue_body:
The new File class contains a dictionary that maps DataFutures for each site to which it is being staged and contains a reference to the DataManager. Neither of these are pickle-able.

So if we do something like this :+1: 
```
data = File("foo.txt")
fu = remote_app(inputs=[data])
fu.result() # <--- We'll get an error from here
```
Here's the relevant piece from the exception traceback :
```
File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages/ipyparallel/serialize/serialize.py", line 112, in serialize_object
   buffers.insert(0, pickle.dumps(cobj, PICKLE_PROTOCOL))
TypeError: can't pickle _thread.lock objects
```

I believe that the File object is the best place to hold the Future information about itself, and that would give us the opportunity to do smarter file staging in the future. So I propose that we fix this with a custom pickler for the File class.

This is blocker for 0.5.0.

issue_title: DateTime Fields should always have a timezone
issue_body:
#### Issue Checklist:
- [X] Descriptive title
- [X] Completed sections below

### Problem Description
We want to ensure that regardless of how the datetime fields are set - from source data, or computed, we want to explicitly set / convert the date to UTC such that the datetime object contains the timezone information.  This ensures consistency of time handling across the system.

### Solution Proposal
Create a `DateTimeField` object derived from `Field` - to be loaded dynamically by the loader.
In the `DateTimeField`, override the `__setattribute__` function to ensure that when 'value' is set, if the datetime object does not contain timezone, then convert the datetime to UTC. 


### Impact on current behavior
None

LINGER Not Working for Device
issue_body:
With this simple STREAMER device, sometimes it will print that it receives all 1000 messages, sometimes it will be ~300, sometimes more or less -- it's so intermittent as to how many messages it receives.

https://gist.github.com/1302163

However, if you uncomment the time.sleep(5) line at the end of the client(), it keeps the client open long enough for the socket to finish sending the messages to the worker and prints that it received all 1000 messages.

This looks like LINGER may not be working in the device.

It usually runs fine the first time, but subsequent times it comes up short:

$ python device_test.py
...
0 got message! #734
0 got message! #735
0 got message! #736
0 got message! #737
0 got message! #738

I have tested this with pyzmq 2.1.9 and the latest GitHub version on both Fedora and Ubuntu with ZMQ 2.1.4 and 2.1.11.
